title: Scenario Comparison — Policy Sensitivity Analysis
use_case: Compare how three policy scenarios shift suitability rankings across Ganga Basin locations. Demonstrates the scenario simulation layer for decision-maker use.

high_logistics.description: Logistics infrastructure is primary decision factor

env_priority.description: Environmental quality elevated — green corridor policy

relaxed_logistics.description: Soft logistics constraint bypassed for planned future infrastructure

kanpur_industrial_zone.reason: Extreme water pollution (WQI=18) is a hard constraint — no scenario can override it

allahabad_confluence.high_logistics: REJECTED or LOW (low logistics_access × 0.50 weight = severe penalty)
allahabad_confluence.relaxed_logistics: HIGHER — soft logistics penalty waived, good river + water quality scores dominate
allahabad_confluence.key_insight: Allahabad is the most scenario-sensitive location — best demonstrates simulation value

patna_river_port.reason: Consistently strong across all factors — scenario-resilient

demo_flow.step_2: Show delta table: which locations changed level, and why
demo_flow.step_3: Focus on allahabad_confluence — most sensitive location
demo_flow.step_4: Show kanpur stays REJECTED across all scenarios — non-negotiable hard constraint
demo_flow.step_5: Render three map layers side by side — color shift reveals policy sensitivity
demo_flow.step_6: Present scenario_metadata for each — always explicit about what changed

talking_points: Hard constraints are non-negotiable — Kanpur is rejected in every scenario
Allahabad is the most sensitive location — a good candidate for targeted infrastructure investment
Patna is scenario-resilient — safe primary investment regardless of policy direction
relaxed_logistics reveals hidden potential — shows what becomes viable with planned infrastructure
The system never silently overrides — every constraint bypass is logged in scenario_metadata
Decision-makers can use this to stress-test rankings before committing to infrastructure investment
